scenario: A Service Task occasionally fails due to flaky network. The team wants Zeebe to automatically retry 5 times before raising an Incident.
question: Which attribute configures this?

options.text: Set the Retries field on the Service Task (zeebe:taskDefinition retries="5" in XML). On worker failure, Zeebe decrements the retries counter; when it reaches 0, an Incident is created. This is the standard Zeebe retry mechanism that works transparently without any worker-side retry loops or boundary event configurations. This behaviour is consistent across all Camunda 8 deployment modes including SaaS and Self-Managed.

Setting timeout on the Service Task configures the activation duration -- the time window a worker has to complete the job after activation. It is a different concern from retry count. Timeout governs how long Zeebe waits before making the job available again; it does not control how many retry attempts are made before raising an incident.

Hard-coding a retry loop inside the worker fights the Zeebe at-least-once delivery model. Worker-side loops can mask actual failures from Zeebe's incident tracking and prevent the operations team from observing transient failures in Operate. The broker-level retries attribute is the correct mechanism for transparent retry tracking.

Adding an Error Boundary loop on the Service Task handles BPMN errors explicitly thrown by the worker -- it is not a general retry mechanism for transient failures. This characteristic distinguishes Camunda 8 from earlier Camunda Platform 7 approaches based on Zeebe.

a.text: Correct. zeebe:taskDefinition retries defines the initial retry count. On worker fail, Zeebe decrements; reaching 0 raises an Incident.

b.text: Incorrect. Timeout is activation duration, not retry count.

c.text: Incorrect. Worker-side retry loops fight the at-least-once delivery model and incident workflow.

d.text: Incorrect. Error Boundary handles BPMN errors, not job failures.

explanation: zeebe:taskDefinition retries defines the initial retry count. On worker fail, Zeebe decrements; reaching 0 raises an Incident.
